checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=615000000 (2009-04-03) growing to OtherAssetsNoncurrent=773000000 (2010-04-02), +25.7%, including a jump from OtherAssetsNoncurrent=528000000 (2010-01-01) to 773000000 (2010-04-02) in a single quarter (+46%)
Revenues=16128000000 (FY2010) vs Revenues=16740000000 (FY2009), a -3.7% decline over the same period
Goodwill=3784000000 (2009-04-03) to Goodwill=3866000000 (2010-04-02), +2.2% despite falling revenue

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=1115000000 (FY2009: 2008-03-29 to 2009-04-03) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=1986000000 (FY2009), OCF exceeds NI
NetIncomeLoss=817000000 (FY2010: 2009-04-04 to 2010-04-02) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=1643000000 (FY2010), OCF exceeds NI

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No inventory concept reported (service-oriented business); CostOfServices=13150000000 (FY2008) vs Revenues=16500000000 (FY2008) implies ~20.3% margin; CostOfServices=13268000000 (FY2009) vs Revenues=16740000000 (FY2009) implies ~20.7% margin; CostOfServices=12797000000 (FY2010) vs Revenues=16128000000 (FY2010) implies ~20.7% margin - stable trend

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: ProfitLoss=559000000 (FY2008) then ProfitLoss=1123000000 (FY2009, +101%) then ProfitLoss=834000000 (FY2010, -26%) - earnings are highly volatile, not smooth, relative to Revenues which moved only +1.5%/-3.7% over the same periods

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=3786000000 (2009-04-03) and AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=3849000000 (2010-04-02) exactly match ReceivablesNetCurrent for the same dates, while AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=55000000 (2009-07-03) is far smaller - the series appears mistagged/inconsistent, preventing reliable trend analysis of the reserve

AccountsPayableCurrent declined from AccountsPayableCurrent=636000000 (2009-04-03) to AccountsPayableCurrent=409000000 (2010-04-02), a -35.7% decline vs Revenues decline of only -3.7% over the same period, while AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent grew from 1264000000 (2009-04-03) to 1344000000 (2010-04-02)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-Q filed 2006-11-09, NT 10-Q filed 2007-02-08, NT 10-K filed 2007-05-30, NT 10-Q filed 2007-08-08, NT 10-Q filed 2007-11-08, followed by a 10-K/A filed 2008-01-11 concurrently with a 10-Q

Repeated CORRESP and UPLOAD filings between 2006 and 2009 (e.g., UPLOAD 2006-08-02, CORRESP 2006-09-01, CORRESP 2007-08-03, CORRESP 2008-01-18, CORRESP 2009-02-25) indicate extended SEC staff review/comment correspondence during this period

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Balance sheet items such as ReceivablesNetCurrent, Goodwill, OtherAssetsNoncurrent, AccountsPayableCurrent are only available for five instant dates from 2009-04-03 to 2010-04-02, and income/cash-flow annual data cover only three fiscal years (FY2008-FY2010), with no inventory, segment, or detailed reserve roll-forward data provided

top_signals: Historical late-filing pattern (multiple NT 10-Q/NT 10-K filings in 2006-2007) and a 10-K/A amendment in January 2008, alongside recurring SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence through 2009, indicating a past period of reporting/control difficulty (likely already remediated by the 2010 cutoff)
OtherAssetsNoncurrent grew ~25.7% (615M to 773M) with a sharp 46% single-quarter jump in Q4 FY2010, while revenue declined ~3.7% year-over-year, an unexplained divergence in a 'soft' asset category

Data quality issue in the AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent series (values equal to gross receivables in two periods) prevents verification of reserve adequacy trends, and AccountsPayableCurrent fell 35.7% versus a much smaller revenue decline

Earnings quality signals are otherwise reassuring: operating cash flow persistently and materially exceeds net income (e.g., OCF=1643M vs NI=817M in FY2010), and margins/receivables-to-revenue relationships are broadly stable, arguing against classic revenue-recognition or cost-deferral manipulation in the most recent filings
